documents.content: In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Establish understanding of the language and processes of counting by naming numbers in sequences, initially to and from 20, moving from any starting point
In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Connect number names, numerals and quantities, including zero, initially up to 10 and then beyond
In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Subitise small collections of objects

In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Compare, order and make correspondences between collections, initially to 20, and explain reasoning
In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Represent practical situations to model addition and sharing

In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Sort and classify familiar objects and explain the basis for these classifications. Copy, continue and create patterns with objects and drawings

In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Use direct and indirect comparisons to decide which is longer, heavier or holds more, and explain reasoning in everyday language
In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Compare and order duration of events using everyday language of time
In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Connect days of the week to familiar events and actions
In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Shape), students learn to: Sort, describe and name familiar two-dimensional shapes and three-dimensional objects in the environment
In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Describe position and movement
In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Answer yes/no questions to collect information and make simple inferences
In Year 1 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Measure and compare the lengths and capacities of pairs of objects using uniform informal units
In Year 1 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Tell time to the half-hour
In Year 1 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Describe duration using months, weeks, days and hours
In Year 1 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Shape), students learn to: Recognise and classify familiar two-dimensional shapes and three-dimensional objects using obvious features
In Year 1 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Give and follow directions to familiar locations

In Year 1 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Develop confidence with number sequences to and from 100 by ones from any starting point. Skip count by twos, fives and tens starting from zero
In Year 1 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Recognise, model, read, write and order numbers to at least 100. Locate these numbers on a number line
In Year 1 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Count collections to 100 by partitioning numbers using place value

In Year 1 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Represent and solve simple addition and subtraction problems using a range of strategies including counting on, partitioning and rearranging parts
In Year 1 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Recognise and describe one-half as one of two equal parts of a whole.
In Year 1 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Money and financial mathematics), students learn to: Recognise, describe and order Australian coins according to their value
In Year 1 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Investigate and describe number patterns formed by skip-counting and patterns with objects

In Year 1 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Identify outcomes of familiar events involving chance and describe them using everyday language such as ‘will happen’, ‘won’t happen’ or ‘might happen’
In Year 1 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Choose simple questions and gather responses and make simple inferences

In Year 1 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Represent data with objects and drawings where one object or drawing represents one data value. Describe the displays

In Year 2 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Compare and order several shapes and objects based on length, area, volume and capacity using appropriate uniform informal units
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Compare masses of objects using balance scales
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Tell time to the quarter-hour, using the language of 'past' and 'to'
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Name and order months and seasons
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Use a calendar to identify the date and determine the number of days in each month
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Shape), students learn to: Describe and draw two-dimensional shapes, with and without digital technologies
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Shape), students learn to: Describe the features of three-dimensional objects
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Interpret simple maps of familiar locations and identify the relative positions of key features
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Investigate the effect of one-step slides and flips with and without digital technologies
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Identify and describe half and quarter turns

In Year 2 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Identify practical activities and everyday events that involve chance. Describe outcomes as ‘likely’ or ‘unlikely’ and identify some events as ‘certain’ or ‘impossible’

In Year 2 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Identify a question of interest based on one categorical variable. Gather data relevant to the question
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Collect, check and classify data
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Create displays of data using lists, table and picture graphs and interpret them

In Year 2 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Investigate number sequences, initially those increasing and decreasing by twos, threes, fives and tens from any starting point, then moving to other sequences
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Recognise, model, represent and order numbers to at least 1000

In Year 2 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Group, partition and rearrange collections up to 1000 in hundreds, tens and ones to facilitate more efficient counting
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Explore the connection between addition and subtraction
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Solve simple addition and subtraction problems using a range of efficient mental and written strategies
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Recognise and represent multiplication as repeated addition, groups and arrays

In Year 2 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Recognise and represent division as grouping into equal sets and solve simple problems using these representations
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Recognise and interpret common uses of halves, quarters and eighths of shapes and collections
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Money and financial mathematics), students learn to: Count and order small collections of Australian coins and notes according to their value
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Describe patterns with numbers and identify missing elements
In Year 2 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Solve problems by using number sentences for addition or subtraction
In Year 3 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Conduct chance experiments, identify and describe possible outcomes and recognise variation in results

In Year 3 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Identify questions or issues for categorical variables. Identify data sources and plan methods of data collection and recording

In Year 3 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Collect data, organise into categories and create displays using lists, tables, picture graphs and simple column graphs, with and without the use of digital technologies
In Year 3 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Interpret and compare data displays
In Year 3 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Measure, order and compare objects using familiar metric units of length, mass and capacity
In Year 3 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Tell time to the minute and investigate the relationship between units of time
In Year 3 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Shape), students learn to: Make models of three-dimensional objects and describe key features
In Year 3 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Create and interpret simple grid maps to show position and pathways
In Year 3 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Identify symmetry in the environment
In Year 3 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Identify angles as measures of turn and compare angle sizes in everyday situations
In Year 3 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Investigate the conditions required for a number to be odd or even and identify odd and even numbers
In Year 3 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Recognise, model, represent and order numbers to at least 10 000

In Year 3 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Apply place value to partition, rearrange and regroup numbers to at least 10 000 to assist calculations and solve problems
In Year 3 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Recognise and explain the connection between addition and subtraction

In Year 3 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Recall addition facts for single-digit numbers and related subtraction facts to develop increasingly efficient mental strategies for computation
In Year 3 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Recall multiplication facts of two, three, five and ten and related division facts

In Year 3 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Represent and solve problems involving multiplication using efficient mental and written strategies and appropriate digital technologies
In Year 3 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Model and represent unit fractions including 1/2, 1/4, 1/3, 1/5 and their multiples to a complete whole

In Year 3 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Money and financial mathematics), students learn to: Represent money values in multiple ways and count the change required for simple transactions to the nearest five cents
In Year 3 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Describe, continue, and create number patterns resulting from performing addition or subtraction
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Investigate and use the properties of odd and even numbers
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Recognise, represent and order numbers to at least tens of thousands

In Year 4 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Apply place value to partition, rearrange and regroup numbers to at least tens of thousands to assist calculations and solve problems
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Investigate number sequences involving multiples of 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, and 9
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Recall multiplication facts up to 10 × 10 and related division facts

In Year 4 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Develop efficient mental and written strategies and use appropriate digital technologies for multiplication and for division where there is no remainder
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Investigate equivalent fractions used in contexts

In Year 4 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Count by quarters halves and thirds, including with mixed numerals. Locate and represent these fractions on a number line

In Year 4 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Recognise that the place value system can be extended to tenths and hundredths. Make connections between fractions and decimal notation

In Year 4 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Money and financial mathematics), students learn to: Solve problems involving purchases and the calculation of change to the nearest five cents with and without digital technologies
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Explore and describe number patterns resulting from performing multiplication

In Year 4 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Solve word problems by using number sentences involving multiplication or division where there is no remainder

In Year 4 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Find unknown quantities in number sentences involving addition and subtraction and identify equivalent number sentences involving addition and subtraction
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Use scaled instruments to measure and compare lengths, masses, capacities and temperatures
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Compare objects using familiar metric units of area and volume
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Convert between units of time
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Use ‘am’ and ‘pm’ notation and solve simple time problems
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Shape), students learn to: Compare the areas of regular and irregular shapes by informal means

In Year 4 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Shape), students learn to: Compare and describe two dimensional shapes that result from combining and splitting common shapes, with and without the use of digital technologies
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Use simple scales, legends and directions to interpret information contained in basic maps
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Create symmetrical patterns, pictures and shapes with and without digital technologies
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Compare angles and classify them as equal to, greater than, or less than, a right angle
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Describe possible everyday events and order their chances of occurring
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Identify everyday events where one cannot happen if the other happens
In Year 4 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Identify events where the chance of one will not be affected by the occurrence of the other

In Year 4 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Select and trial methods for data collection, including survey questions and recording sheets

In Year 4 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Construct suitable data displays, with and without the use of digital technologies, from given or collected data. Include tables, column graphs and picture graphs where one picture can represent many data values

In Year 4 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Evaluate the effectiveness of different displays in illustrating data features including variability

In Year 5 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: List outcomes of chance experiments involving equally likely outcomes and represent probabilities of those outcomes using fractions
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Recognise that probabilities range from 0 to 1
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Pose questions and collect categorical or numerical data by observation or survey

In Year 5 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Construct displays, including column graphs, dot plots and tables, appropriate for data type, with and without the use of digital technologies
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Describe and interpret different data sets in context
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Choose appropriate units of measurement for length, area, volume, capacity and mass
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Calculate perimeter and area of rectangles using familiar metric units
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Compare 12- and 24-hour time systems and convert between them
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Shape), students learn to: Connect three-dimensional objects with their nets and other two-dimensional representations

In Year 5 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Use a grid reference system to describe locations. Describe routes using landmarks and directional language

In Year 5 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Describe translations, reflections and rotations of two-dimensional shapes. Identify line and rotational symmetries

In Year 5 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Apply the enlargement transformation to familiar two dimensional shapes and explore the properties of the resulting image compared with the original
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Estimate, measure and compare angles using degrees. Construct angles using a protractor
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Identify and describe factors and multiples of whole numbers and use them to solve problems
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Use estimation and rounding to check the reasonableness of answers to calculations

In Year 5 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Solve problems involving multiplication of large numbers by one- or two-digit numbers using efficient mental, written strategies and appropriate digital technologies
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Solve problems involving division by a one digit number, including those that result in a remainder
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Use efficient mental and written strategies and apply appropriate digital technologies to solve problems
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Compare and order common unit fractions and locate and represent them on a number line

In Year 5 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Investigate strategies to solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions with the same denominator
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Recognise that the place value system can be extended beyond hundredths
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Compare, order and represent decimals
In Year 5 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Money and financial mathematics), students learn to: Create simple financial plans

In Year 5 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Describe, continue and create patterns with fractions, decimals and whole numbers resulting from addition and subtraction

In Year 5 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Find unknown quantities in number sentences involving multiplication and division and identify equivalent number sentences involving multiplication and division
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Describe probabilities using fractions, decimals and percentages
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Conduct chance experiments with both small and large numbers of trials using appropriate digital technologies
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Compare observed frequencies across experiments with expected frequencies

In Year 6 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Interpret and compare a range of data displays, including side-by-side column graphs for two categorical variables
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Interpret secondary data presented in digital media and elsewhere
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Identify and describe properties of prime, composite, square and triangular numbers

In Year 6 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Select and apply efficient mental and written strategies and appropriate digital technologies to solve problems involving all four operations with whole numbers
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Investigate everyday situations that use integers. Locate and represent these numbers on a number line
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Compare fractions with related denominators and locate and represent them on a number line
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions with the same or related denominators

In Year 6 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Find a simple fraction of a quantity where the result is a whole number, with and without digital technologies

In Year 6 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Add and subtract decimals, with and without digital technologies, and use estimation and rounding to check the reasonableness of answers

In Year 6 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Multiply decimals by whole numbers and perform divisions by non-zero whole numbers where the results are terminating decimals, with and without digital technologies
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Multiply and divide decimals by powers of 10
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Fractions and decimals), students learn to: Make connections between equivalent fractions, decimals and percentages

In Year 6 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Money and financial mathematics), students learn to: Investigate and calculate percentage discounts of 10%, 25% and 50% on sale items, with and without digital technologies

In Year 6 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Continue and create sequences involving whole numbers, fractions and decimals. Describe the rule used to create the sequence
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Explore the use of brackets and order of operations to write number sentences
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Connect decimal representations to the metric system
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Convert between common metric units of length, mass and capacity
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Solve problems involving the comparison of lengths and areas using appropriate units
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Connect volume and capacity and their units of measurement
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Interpret and use timetables
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Shape), students learn to: Construct simple prisms and pyramids

In Year 6 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Investigate combinations of translations, reflections and rotations, with and without the use of digital technologies
In Year 6 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Introduce the Cartesian coordinate system using all four quadrants

In Year 6 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Investigate, with and without digital technologies, angles on a straight line, angles at a point and vertically opposite angles. Use results to find unknown angles

In Year 7 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Establish the formulas for areas of rectangles, triangles and parallelograms, and use these in problem-solving
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Calculate volumes of rectangular prisms
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Shape), students learn to: Draw different views of prisms and solids formed from combinations of prisms

In Year 7 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Location and transformation), students learn to: Describe translations, reflections in an axis and rotations of multiples of 90° on the Cartesian plane using coordinates. Identify line and rotational symmetries
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Classify triangles according to their side and angle properties and describe quadrilaterals

In Year 7 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Demonstrate that the angle sum of a triangle is 180° and use this to find the angle sum of a quadrilateral

In Year 7 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Identify corresponding, alternate and co-interior angles when two straight lines are crossed by a transversal
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Investigate conditions for two lines to be parallel and solve simple numerical problems using reasoning
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Construct sample spaces for single-step experiments with equally likely outcomes
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Assign probabilities to the outcomes of events and determine probabilities for events

In Year 7 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Identify and investigate issues involving numerical data collected from primary and secondary sources

In Year 7 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Construct and compare a range of data displays including stem-and-leaf plots and dot plots

In Year 7 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Calculate mean, median, mode and range for sets of data. Interpret these statistics in the context of data
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Describe and interpret data displays using median, mean and range
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Investigate index notation and represent whole numbers as products of powers of prime numbers
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Investigate and use square roots of perfect square numbers
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Apply the associative, commutative and distributive laws to aid mental and written computation
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Compare, order, add and subtract integers

In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Compare fractions using equivalence. Locate and represent positive and negative fractions and mixed numbers on a number line
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions, including those with unrelated denominators
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Multiply and divide fractions and decimals using efficient written strategies and digital technologies
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Express one quantity as a fraction of another, with and without the use of digital technologies
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Round decimals to a specified number of decimal places
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Connect fractions, decimals and percentages and carry out simple conversions

In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Find percentages of quantities and express one quantity as a percentage of another, with and without digital technologies.
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Recognise and solve problems involving simple ratios
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Money and financial mathematics), students learn to: Investigate and calculate 'best buys', with and without digital technologies
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Introduce the concept of variables as a way of representing numbers using letters
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Create algebraic expressions and evaluate them by substituting a given value for each variable
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Extend and apply the laws and properties of arithmetic to algebraic terms and expressions
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Given coordinates, plot points on the Cartesian plane, and find coordinates for a given point
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Solve simple linear equations
In Year 7 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Investigate, interpret and analyse graphs from authentic data

In Year 8 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Use index notation with numbers to establish the index laws with positive integral indices and the zero index

In Year 8 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Number and place value), students learn to: Carry out the four operations with rational numbers and integers, using efficient mental and written strategies and appropriate digital technologies
In Year 8 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Investigate terminating and recurring decimals
In Year 8 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Investigate the concept of irrational numbers, including π

In Year 8 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Solve problems involving the use of percentages, including percentage increases and decreases, with and without digital technologies
In Year 8 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Solve a range of problems involving rates and ratios, with and without digital technologies
In Year 8 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Money and financial mathematics), students learn to: Solve problems involving profit and loss, with and without digital technologies
In Year 8 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Extend and apply the distributive law to the expansion of algebraic expressions
In Year 8 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Factorise algebraic expressions by identifying numerical factors
In Year 8 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Simplify algebraic expressions involving the four operations

In Year 8 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Plot linear relationships on the Cartesian plane with and without the use of digital technologies

In Year 8 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Solve linear equations using algebraic and graphical techniques. Verify solutions by substitution
In Year 8 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Identify complementary events and use the sum of probabilities to solve problems

In Year 8 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Describe events using language of 'at least', exclusive 'or' (A or B but not both), inclusive 'or' (A or B or both) and 'and'.
In Year 8 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Represent events in two-way tables and Venn diagrams and solve related problems

In Year 8 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Investigate techniques for collecting data, including census, sampling and observation

In Year 8 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Explore the practicalities and implications of obtaining data through sampling using a variety of investigative processes

In Year 8 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Explore the variation of means and proportions of random samples drawn from the same population

In Year 8 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Investigate the effect of individual data values, including outliers, on the mean and median
In Year 8 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Choose appropriate units of measurement for area and volume and convert from one unit to another
In Year 8 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Find perimeters and areas of parallelograms, trapeziums, rhombuses and kites

In Year 8 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Investigate the relationship between features of circles such as circumference, area, radius and diameter. Use formulas to solve problems involving circumference and area

In Year 8 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Develop formulas for volumes of rectangular and triangular prisms and prisms in general. Use formulas to solve problems involving volume

In Year 8 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Solve problems involving duration, including using 12- and 24-hour time within a single time zone
In Year 8 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Define congruence of plane shapes using transformations
In Year 8 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Develop the conditions for congruence of triangles

In Year 8 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Establish properties of quadrilaterals using congruent triangles and angle properties, and solve related numerical problems using reasoning

In Year 9 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: List all outcomes for two-step chance experiments, both with and without replacement using tree diagrams or arrays. Assign probabilities to outcomes and determine probabilities for events

In Year 9 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Calculate relative frequencies from given or collected data to estimate probabilities of events involving 'and' or 'or'

In Year 9 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Investigate reports of surveys in digital media and elsewhere for information on how data were obtained to estimate population means and medians

In Year 9 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Identify everyday questions and issues involving at least one numerical and at least one categorical variable, and collect data directly and from secondary sources

In Year 9 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Construct back-to-back stem-and-leaf plots and histograms and describe data, using terms including ‘skewed’, ‘symmetric’ and ‘bi modal’

In Year 9 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Compare data displays using mean, median and range to describe and interpret numerical data sets in terms of location (centre) and spread
In Year 9 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Calculate areas of composite shapes
In Year 9 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Calculate the surface area and volume of cylinders and solve related problems
In Year 9 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Solve problems involving the surface area and volume of right prisms
In Year 9 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Investigate very small and very large time scales and intervals

In Year 9 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Use the enlargement transformation to explain similarity and develop the conditions for triangles to be similar
In Year 9 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Solve problems using ratio and scale factors in similar figures

In Year 9 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Pythagoras and trigonometry), students learn to: Investigate Pythagoras’ Theorem and its application to solving simple problems involving right angled triangles

In Year 9 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Pythagoras and trigonometry), students learn to: Use similarity to investigate the constancy of the sine, cosine and tangent ratios for a given angle in right-angled triangles
In Year 9 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Pythagoras and trigonometry), students learn to: Apply trigonometry to solve right-angled triangle problems

In Year 9 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Solve problems involving direct proportion. Explore the relationship between graphs and equations corresponding to simple rate problems
In Year 9 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Apply index laws to numerical expressions with integer indices
In Year 9 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Express numbers in scientific notation
In Year 9 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Money and financial mathematics), students learn to: Solve problems involving simple interest
In Year 9 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Extend and apply the index laws to variables, using positive integer indices and the zero index

In Year 9 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Apply the distributive law to the expansion of algebraic expressions, including binomials, and collect like terms where appropriate

In Year 9 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Find the distance between two points located on the Cartesian plane using a range of strategies, including graphing software

In Year 9 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Find the midpoint and gradient of a line segment (interval) on the Cartesian plane using a range of strategies, including graphing software
In Year 9 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Sketch linear graphs using the coordinates of two points and solve linear equations

In Year 9 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Graph simple non-linear relations with and without the use of digital technologies and solve simple related equations

In Year 10 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Money and financial mathematics), students learn to: Connect the compound interest formula to repeated applications of simple interest using appropriate digital technologies
In Year 10 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Factorise algebraic expressions by taking out a common algebraic factor
In Year 10 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Simplify algebraic products and quotients using index laws
In Year 10 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Apply the four operations to simple algebraic fractions with numerical denominators
In Year 10 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Expand binomial products and factorise monic quadratic expressions using a variety of strategies
In Year 10 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Substitute values into formulas to determine an unknown
In Year 10 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Solve problems involving linear equations, including those derived from formulas
In Year 10 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Solve linear inequalities and graph their solutions on a number line

In Year 10 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Solve linear simultaneous equations, using algebraic and graphical techniques, including using digital technology
In Year 10 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Solve problems involving parallel and perpendicular lines

In Year 10 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Explore the connection between algebraic and graphical representations of relations such as simple quadratics, circles and exponentials using digital technology as appropriate
In Year 10 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Solve linear equations involving simple algebraic fractions
In Year 10 Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Solve simple quadratic equations using a range of strategies

In Year 10 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Solve problems involving surface area and volume for a range of prisms, cylinders and composite solids
In Year 10 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Formulate proofs involving congruent triangles and angle properties

In Year 10 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Apply logical reasoning, including the use of congruence and similarity, to proofs and numerical exercises involving plane shapes

In Year 10 Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Pythagoras and trigonometry), students learn to: Solve right-angled triangle problems including those involving direction and angles of elevation and depression

In Year 10 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Describe the results of two- and three-step chance experiments, both with and without replacements, assign probabilities to outcomes and determine probabilities of events. Investigate the concept of independence

In Year 10 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Use the language of ‘if ....then, ‘given’, ‘of’, ‘knowing that’ to investigate conditional statements and identify common mistakes in interpreting such language
In Year 10 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Determine quartiles and interquartile range
In Year 10 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Construct and interpret box plots and use them to compare data sets
In Year 10 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Compare shapes of box plots to corresponding histograms and dot plots

In Year 10 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Use scatter plots to investigate and comment on relationships between two numerical variables

In Year 10 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Investigate and describe bivariate numerical data where the independent variable is time

In Year 10 Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Evaluate statistical reports in the media and other places by linking claims to displays, statistics and representative data
In Year 10A Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Define rational and irrational numbers and perform operations with surds and fractional indices
In Year 10A Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Real numbers), students learn to: Use the definition of a logarithm to establish and apply the laws of logarithms
In Year 10A Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Patterns and algebra), students learn to: Investigate the concept of a polynomial and apply the factor and remainder theorems to solve problems
In Year 10A Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Solve simple exponential equations

In Year 10A Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Describe, interpret and sketch parabolas, hyperbolas, circles and exponential functions and their transformations

In Year 10A Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Apply understanding of polynomials to sketch a range of curves and describe the features of these curves from their equation

In Year 10A Mathematics, under Number and Algebra (Linear and non-linear relationships), students learn to: Factorise monic and non-monic quadratic expressions and solve a wide range of quadratic equations derived from a variety of contexts

In Year 10A Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Using units of measurement), students learn to: Solve problems involving surface area and volume of right pyramids, right cones, spheres and related composite solids
In Year 10A Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Geometric reasoning), students learn to: Prove and apply angle and chord properties of circles
In Year 10A Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Pythagoras and trigonometry), students learn to: Establish the sine, cosine and area rules for any triangle and solve related problems

In Year 10A Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Pythagoras and trigonometry), students learn to: Use the unit circle to define trigonometric functions, and graph them with and without the use of digital technologies
In Year 10A Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Pythagoras and trigonometry), students learn to: Solve simple trigonometric equations

In Year 10A Mathematics, under Measurement and Geometry (Pythagoras and trigonometry), students learn to: Apply Pythagoras’ Theorem and trigonometry to solving three-dimensional problems in right-angled triangles

In Year 10A Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Investigate reports of studies in digital media and elsewhere for information on their planning and implementation

In Year 10A Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Calculate and interpret the mean and standard deviation of data and use these to compare data sets

In Year 10A Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Use information technologies to investigate bivariate numerical data sets. Where appropriate use a straight line to describe the relationship allowing for variation

In Year 10A Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Use the language of ‘if ....then, ‘given’, ‘of’, ‘knowing that’ to investigate conditional statements and identify common mistakes in interpreting such language
In Year 10A Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Determine quartiles and interquartile range
In Year 10A Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Construct and interpret box plots and use them to compare data sets
In Year 10A Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Compare shapes of box plots to corresponding histograms and dot plots

In Year 10A Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Use scatter plots to investigate and comment on relationships between two numerical variables

In Year 10A Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Investigate and describe bivariate numerical data where the independent variable is time

In Year 10A Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Evaluate statistical reports in the media and other places by linking claims to displays, statistics and representative data

In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Chance), students learn to: Investigate reports of studies in digital media and elsewhere for information on their planning and implementation

In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Calculate and interpret the mean and standard deviation of data and use these to compare data sets

In Foundation Year Mathematics, under Statistics and Probability (Data representation and interpretation), students learn to: Use information technologies to investigate bivariate numerical data sets. Where appropriate use a straight line to describe the relationship allowing for variation
